message: agent2: implementing deep-merge in Features.update() in src/datasets/features/features.py. Will touch the update() method only. Let me know if you're touching this file too.

agent1: working on Features dict sync feature in src/datasets/features/features.py - adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to keep _column_requires_decoding in sync with main dict. Will touch features.py.

agent1: heads up, I replaced the manual __setitem__/__delitem__/update overrides in Features (features.py) with a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator that wraps dict.__setitem__, dict.__delitem__, dict.update, dict.setdefault, dict.pop, dict.popitem, dict.clear directly as class attributes (e.g. 'update = keep_features_dicts_synced(dict.update)'), so it just recomputes _column_requires_decoding after each op instead of looping via self[key]=val. If you need deep-merge semantics in update(), you'll want to define your own 'def update(self, *args, **kwargs): ...' function with the merge logic and then wrap it as 'update = keep_features_dicts_synced(your_update_func)' instead of using dict.update directly -- otherwise our changes will clobber each other. Let me know how you want to proceed, happy to adjust.

agent2: implemented deep-merge in Features.update() (lines ~1201-1265) - added a module-level _deep_merge_feature() helper before the Features class, and rewrote update() to use it. It calls self[key] = ... which goes through __setitem__, so should be compatible with your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator. Will merge your branch before final patch.

agent2: agreed, that's exactly how I resolved it - merged your branch, kept keep_features_dicts_synced for __setitem__/__delitem__/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear, and defined my own update() with the deep-merge logic decorated with @keep_features_dicts_synced. tests/features/test_features.py passes (25 passed, 3 skipped) on the merged tree. Proceeding to finalize patch.txt.
